data.title: Liquibase Unveils Change Intelligence and New Connectors for Governed Database Delivery
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Liquibase, a prominent provider of Database Change Governance solutions, recently introduced Liquibase Change Intelligence and a fresh lineup of Liquibase Secure Deployment Connectors. These additions aim to enhance enterprises' ability to oversee, regulate, and execute database changes within contemporary delivery infrastructures. The innovative features enable teams to grasp alterations within their databases, track delivery efficiency, detect potential risks sooner, address problems with up to 95% quicker resolution time, and consolidate audit data.

data.title: e.l.f. Cosmetics Cracks the Case on Vanity Vandals, the Latest Addition in its Cosmetic Crime Series
data.description: In Oakland, California – A new case of makeup chaos has emerged as e.l.f. Cosmetics, a brand under e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE: ELF), launches a mockumentary titled Vanity Vandals. The film delves into the e.l.f. Cosmetics phenomenon causing a craze in households worldwide. Dubbed as "vanity vandalism" by e.l.f., this trend involves the overwhelming presence of affordable beauty products on vanities. The bold disruptor company aims to shed light on this issue with the release of Vanity Vandals.

data.title: J.C. Flowers & Co. Announces Investment in Niyam Group and Niyam Syndicate 2047
data.description: A new Indian reinsurance company with an AA- rating from Lloyd's has been set up in Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City), which is India's sole specialized international financial services hub. This development, announced on March 31, 2026, marks a collaboration between J.C. Flowers & Co. from London and MUMBAI, with significant investment and operational presence in the region. This initiative is poised to bring considerable financial opportunities and bolster the reinsurance sector, leveraging the unique capabilities of GIFT City in India.

data.title: Trump Said Willing To End Iran War Without Strait Of Hormuz Reopening; Market Jumps
data.description: According to a report from the Wall Street Journal, President Donald Trump has informed his advisors that he is prepared to bring an end to the conflict with Iran, even if the vital oil shipping route, the Strait of Hormuz, stays closed. This news has had a positive impact on the market, leading to a notable increase in market activity as a result.

data.title: Paladin Acknowledges Judicial Review Application of EIS Approval
data.description: In Perth, Australia, on March 30, 2026, Paladin Energy Ltd (ASX:PDN, TSX:PDN, OTCQX:PALAF) received notice that the Métis Nation–Saskatchewan (MN – S) has filed for a judicial review in the Saskatchewan Court of King’s Bench. This challenge is in response to the Saskatchewan Minister of Environment's approval of the Company’s Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) for the Patterson Lake South (PLS) Project 1, a decision made on February 19, 2026.

data.title: Senators urge SEC for answers after chief's exit; dispute over Trump-linked cases. Justin Sun's situation also under scrutiny.
data.description: Earlier this month, a report stated that the former chief of enforcement at the SEC had disagreements with agency leadership over cases related to individuals associated with Trump. Following these clashes, the chief resigned from the SEC. The report from Reuters highlighted this incident, which occurred amidst increasing regulatory scrutiny.

data.title: Valley County Approves C-PACE Program to Support Commercial Development and Investment at Tamarack Resort and Across the Region
data.description: In Donnelly, Idaho on March 30, 2026, Valley County successfully passed the adoption of a Commercial Property Assessed Capital Expenditure (C-PACE) program. This initiative introduces a novel financing mechanism aimed at bolstering commercial growth, fostering energy efficiency projects, and encouraging lasting investments within the region. This decision is anticipated to stimulate economic activities and promote sustainable development within Valley County.

data.title: Iran, Russia Using Crypto To Buy Military Drones? Report Reveals E-Commerce Platforms Used To Sell Equipment To 'Opaque' Identities
data.description: A recent report revealed that actors associated with Russia and Iran are utilizing cryptocurrency to purchase inexpensive drones for various conflicts. The report, released on Monday, highlights how sanctioned entities, particularly Iran's defense sector and its allies, are utilizing cryptocurrency to acquire drone components that are limited by export regulations. One example cited is Iran's Shahed drone program, which heavily relies on components obtained through third-party procurement networks. According to a study conducted by Chainalysis, cryptocurrency is being used in two main ways for procurement: direct purchases where drone manufacturers accept cryptocurrency as payment, and indirect purchases where vendors on third-party e-commerce platforms like Alibaba Group Holding Limited accept cryptocurrency. This method has been particularly beneficial for entities involved in conflicts with the U.S. and Israel. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
